behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than configured threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git ancestry/age computation plus PR-state join. MOCK-MISS: this is not one-function logic; it is git-state + PR-state interaction. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: bugs in git command usage, branch-tip resolution, or timestamp extraction are hidden by pure mocks. No LLM dependence, no multi-agent chain. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is purely deterministic git/PR logic.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, create divergent branch commits with controlled commit dates, simulate/point to PR state provider indicating no open PR, run detector end-to-end through actual git subprocess calls, and assert stale=true only when all three predicates hold.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at bootstrap/runtime boundary: startup wiring must invoke detection and emit warning in real session context. MOCK-MISS: unit/integration tests can pass while startup hook registration or environment-dependent branch resolution fails. REAL-INFRA: session bootstrap and current-branch discovery typically use process/env/git calls. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because no model decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Launch session bootstrap path in a temp repo on a known stale branch, capture emitted startup output/log stream, and assert warning appears with branch name and actionable context fields.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly a local deterministic mapping function. MOCK-MISS: no module boundary is required to reproduce failure. REAL-INFRA: none. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration+ because behavior text says "local status-classifier function" and deterministic label mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven test of classifier inputs (computed branch-state structs) to expected labels, including edge cases, asserting pure deterministic output with no I/O.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes clean from stale-no-PR in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires "against live repo and PR state"; lower levels with stubs can hide real git/API mismatches and rendering from real data. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both apply (real repo state + real PR service interaction). No LLM dependence or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` in an environment wired to a real test repo and real PR state source, exercise both clean and stale-no-pr scenarios, and assert rendered output text/classification matches expected state.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is module handoff between staleness logic and PR-status interpretation (open/merged should suppress stale-no-pr). In-process fakes for PR states can reproduce this exact bug, so System is not strictly required. Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess proven by behavior text; (b) no AI output; (c) no multi-agent chain. Rejected System because behavior text does not require live infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire detector + PR-state adapter with in-process test doubles/data fixtures for open and merged PR states, run classification pipeline, and assert stale-no-pr is never emitted for those states.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Concrete failure is tied to real git history transformations (rebase/squash) and ancestry semantics after hash changes. MOCK-HIDE: synthetic fixtures often miss git graph corner cases that real git operations expose. REAL-INFRA: requires real git subprocess/repo manipulation. Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly centers on rebase/squash commit-hash changes, a real-VCS behavior boundary.
behaviors.test_description: In a temporary real git repo, create stale branch, then perform actual rebase and squash-merge workflows that rewrite hashes; rerun detector and assert branch is no longer classified as stale-no-pr when work is effectively represented in main/PR history.
